title: Stop a delivery from quietly ending with its work unmerged or unarchived
summary: A karta delivery has two hand-offs that happen after the interesting work is done: the orchestrator merges every built item, and a finished run archives its binder. Both live only as skill prose today, so an orchestrator under context pressure can end its turn with built refs still standing or a complete binder never archived, and nothing below the agent catches it. This adds a sixth Claude Code hook that watches for a session trying to stop in one of those two states, blocks that stop once with the exact fix named, then lets the next identical stop through — a nudge that keeps a dirty delivery from slipping past unannounced without turning into a wall you can't get past.
motivation: The merge-queue and archive hand-offs at the end of a delivery live only as skill prose today; this moves a check below the agent so a session cannot quietly end with built-but-unmerged items or a complete-but-unarchived binder.

included: A new Stop-event plugin guard hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py — the sixth hook, fifth guard — that blocks a session's stop once when a live binder has built-but-unmerged items or is complete-but-unarchived, and is fail-open (corrective, not fail-closed)
A new hooks/hooks.json Stop entry (matcher `*`, timeout 30) that runs the guard, plus an extension of the file's top-level description string to name the sixth concern

docs/how-to/hooks.md — a new enforcement-table row ('A delivery may not end dirty'), the plugin-hook count updated from five to six, and a Codex-asymmetry sentence (Codex has no Stop surface, doctrine unchanged there)
README.md — the delivery-end clause added to the 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph's rule list, with the session-start parenthetical renumbered to sixth
excluded: SubagentStop whiff-worker detection — a wave worker ending with neither `built` nor `failed`; feasible because the SubagentStop payload carries `agent_type`, but a separate design out of scope here

Codex parity — Codex has no Stop surface upstream, so its delivery-end doctrine stays skill prose; the guard is written runtime-agnostic so wiring it into a future Codex Stop surface would need no rewrite

Any gate on `failed` refs — a halted item parked at the four-way human choice is a designed resting state already surfaced by the session-start injection and `karta_next.py`, so it is deliberately not a finding
Any session-intent inference from `last_assistant_message` prose or the transcript — text-sniffing is exactly the nondeterminism karta exists to avoid

Any change to the five existing guards (guard_binder_immutability.py, guard_pack_write.py, guard_auditor_dispatch.py, guard_writer_confinement.py, inject_karta_status.py) — the new guard composes with them and answers a different question; none is modified

design_facts.stack: karta plugin repo — Python 3.11+ stdlib scripts run via `uv run --script`, each with an embedded `--self-test` ([PASS]/[FAIL] + `N/N checks passed`); Markdown SKILL.md skills with 3-way byte-identical mirrors (skills/ -> .agents/skills/ -> plugins/karta/skills/) regenerated by sync_codex_skills.py; JSON binder files. Ships as a Claude Code plugin and a Codex CLI plugin.

work_items.title: Catch a delivery that tries to stop before its work is merged and archived
work_items.summary: This lands the stdlib Stop guard with its full self-test suite and the hooks.json entry that runs it on every session stop — as one change, since the plugin validator rejects both an unreferenced script and a dangling reference.
work_items.contract: New Stop-event plugin guard hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py in the house pattern of the five shipped guards: pure stdlib, argparse, PEP 723 header (requires-python >=3.11, no dependencies), a `--self-test` mode printing `[PASS]`/`[FAIL]` lines and an `N/N checks passed` summary, hook payload JSON on stdin, exit 0 allows and exit 2 blocks with a one-paragraph reason on stderr. Unlike guard_auditor_dispatch.py and guard_writer_confinement.py this guard is fail-open and corrective, not fail-closed: any genuinely unexpected internal error (no git binary, unreadable payload, unreadable binder, missing fields) exits 0, because a stray Stop trap is strictly worse than a missed nudge. Decision logic, in order: (1) process only payloads whose `hook_event_name` is exactly `Stop` and that carry no subagent fields — anything else, a SubagentStop-shaped payload included, exits 0. (2) If `stop_hook_active` is true, exit 0 (the harness-documented loop guard). (3) Resolve the repo root with `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` from the payload `cwd` (a session may sit in a subdirectory), and read every git fact through plumbing — `git for-each-ref`, `git cat-file`, `git ls-tree` — never by inspecting `.git/refs/` files, which go silent once refs are packed. (4) Enumerate live binders as the union of `.karta/binders/*.json` in the working tree and in `HEAD` (via `git ls-tree`), never under `archive/` — a delivered slug's surviving refs are history, not a finding; HEAD inclusion matters because a crash between the archive `git mv` and its commit removes the live file from disk while the archival is not yet real. For each live slug, test two conditions. built-unmerged: some `refs/karta/<slug>/item-<id>/built` ref, enumerated by ref glob (not from the binder's `work_items`, so an orphaned ref still counts), has no matching `done` and no matching `failed`; the ¬failed clause keeps a merge-time halt that left `built` standing — a parked four-way-choice state — from re-firing the gate. complete-unarchived: the binder has at least one work item (an empty `work_items` is vacuously complete and never a finding), every item has a `done` ref, and the archive file `.karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json` is not committed anywhere it counts — not in `HEAD`, not on `refs/heads/karta/<slug>/integration`, and not on the default branch. An archive file merely present on disk or staged but uncommitted does not count — doctrine requires a commit; an archive commit sitting on the integration branch awaiting human review/merge is the correct end state and is no finding. `git cat-file -e` / missing-branch negatives are expected results that feed the finding, not internal errors — the fail-open path is reserved for genuinely unexpected failures, never for `ref not found`. Deliberately not conditions: a standing `failed` ref (a designed resting state), a partially-delivered binder with no `built` standing, and anything inferred from `last_assistant_message` prose. Block-once mechanics — on a finding the guard blocks at most once per (session, state): compute a state fingerprint = SHA-256 over the sorted `(slug, condition, item-ids)` tuples; read the sentinel `<git-common-dir>/karta-stop-gate.json` (per-repo, shared across worktrees) mapping `session_id` -> fingerprint, and if this `session_id` already maps to this fingerprint exit 0 (already nudged for exactly this state); otherwise record `session_id -> fingerprint` — pruned to 20 sessions, written atomically via a temp file + `os.replace` — and exit 2. The sentinel is written only on a block, so a partial fix inside a stop-hook continuation defers the nudge one turn rather than losing it. The exit-2 reason names each finding and its fix verbatim: for built-unmerged, `binder <slug>: items <ids> carry built but no done — the serial merge queue did not finish. Re-enter karta-deliver's merge step: for each, re-validate its oracle against the current integration tip, merge FIFO, write the done ref; or tell the user plainly that the delivery is stopping mid-wave and how to resume.`; for complete-unarchived, `binder <slug>: all items are done but the binder was never archived. Run the end-of-life step (deliver:archive / karta-build 9c-single): git mv it to .karta/binders/archive/<slug>.json and commit on the integration branch.` The gate is a nudge, not a wall: a changed state (new fingerprint) blocks once more, an unchanged state never re-fires in that session, and the harness block-cap backstops all of it. Ships every fixture in the design's self-test table: no live binder -> allow; archived-only slug with surviving refs -> allow; in-flight binder with no `built` standing -> allow; built-unmerged -> block naming slug+items; item with `built` and `failed`, no `done` -> allow (parked); empty `work_items` -> allow; complete-unarchived with no archive anywhere -> block; complete-unarchived with no integration branch at all -> block (missing branch is a negative, not an error); archive `git mv`'d but uncommitted -> block; complete with archive committed on the integration branch only -> allow; complete with archive in `HEAD` -> allow; archive merged to the default branch under a stale feature-branch checkout -> allow; two live binders with mixed findings -> one block whose fingerprint covers both; payload `cwd` in a subdirectory -> still detects; SubagentStop-shaped payload -> allow; `stop_hook_active` true -> allow; same session + same fingerprint -> allow on the second call; same session + changed fingerprint -> block again; new session + same state -> block once; malformed binder JSON -> allow (fail-open); non-git cwd -> allow; sentinel pruning at 20 sessions. hooks/hooks.json gains one `Stop` entry with matcher `*`, timeout 30, running `${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py`, and the file's top-level `description` string is extended to name the sixth concern (the delivery Stop-gate) alongside the existing five. Both files land together: validate_plugin.py errors both on a hooks/scripts/ script that hooks.json does not reference and on a reference to a missing script, so they are not separately mergeable.

oracle.command: uv run --script hooks/scripts/guard_delivery_stop.py --self-test && uv run scripts/validate_plugin.py --self-test
assertions: guard_delivery_stop.py --self-test exits 0 and prints an `N/N checks passed` summary with every fixture [PASS] and no [FAIL]
no live binder, an archived-only slug with surviving refs, an in-flight binder with no `built` standing, and an empty `work_items` all allow (nothing dirty to catch)
built-unmerged blocks with a reason naming the slug and the item ids, while an item carrying both `built` and `failed` with no `done` allows (a parked four-way-choice state, not a finding)

complete-unarchived blocks when the archive is committed nowhere that counts — including with no integration branch at all (a missing branch is an expected negative, not an internal error) and when the archive is `git mv`'d but uncommitted — while a complete binder whose archive is committed on the integration branch, present in `HEAD`, or merged to the default branch under a stale feature-branch checkout each allow

repo root is resolved via `git rev-parse --show-toplevel` so a payload `cwd` in a subdirectory still detects, a SubagentStop-shaped payload and `stop_hook_active` true both allow, and two live binders with mixed findings produce one block whose fingerprint covers both

block-once per (session, state): a second stop with the same fingerprint allows, a changed fingerprint blocks again, and a new session with the same state blocks once; the sentinel is written only on a block and pruned to 20 sessions

fail-open holds — malformed binder JSON and a non-git cwd both allow (exit 0) — and hooks.json gains one `Stop` entry (matcher `*`, timeout 30) pointing at the guard with its top-level description naming the sixth concern; validate_plugin --self-test passes, executing the guard's self-test and confirming the script is referenced

work_items.title: Explain the delivery Stop-gate in the hooks how-to
work_items.summary: Readers of the hooks guide learn that a delivery can't quietly stop while it is unmerged or unarchived, that the block is a one-time nudge rather than a wall, and why Codex has no equivalent.
work_items.contract: docs/how-to/hooks.md gets three edits. First, a new row in the 'What is enforced, and where' table — 'A delivery may not end dirty', enforced by a Claude Code plugin hook: built-but-unmerged items (a `built` ref with no `done`) or a complete-but-unarchived binder block the session's stop once, with the fix named in the reason; a second stop with the same state passes — a nudge, not a wall. Second, the count sentence under the table ('The first five ship in the plugin ... The last two live in this repository') is updated from five to six, since the new row is a sixth plugin hook. Third, a Codex-asymmetry sentence in the 'Why Claude Code and Codex differ (for now)' section: Codex has no Stop surface upstream, so its delivery-end doctrine stays skill prose and does not regress, and the guard_delivery_stop.py script is written runtime-agnostic like the others, so backing a future Codex Stop surface with it would need no rewrite. The 'How a hook decides' internal-error paragraph does NOT change its fail-closed enumeration — this guard is fail-open and corrective, so it is deliberately not added to the two fail-closed guards (guard_auditor_dispatch.py, guard_writer_confinement.py); at most the paragraph's description of hook events may gain a passing mention of the Stop event, but keep edits minimal per spec. Prose stays plain-language, consistent with the doctrine wording used in the design and the guard.

assertions: the 'What is enforced, and where' table has a new 'A delivery may not end dirty' row describing that built-but-unmerged items or a complete-but-unarchived binder block the session's stop once with the fix in the reason, and that a second same-state stop passes
the count sentence under the table now reads six plugin hooks (not five) with the last two still living in this repository

a Codex-asymmetry sentence states Codex has no Stop surface so its delivery-end doctrine stays prose there, and names guard_delivery_stop.py as runtime-agnostic so a later Codex Stop surface would need no rewrite
the 'How a hook decides' fail-closed enumeration is unchanged — the new guard is not added to the two fail-closed guards, and it stays described as fail-open corrective

work_items.title: Add the delivery-end rule to the README enforcement list
work_items.summary: A newcomer skimming the README sees that a delivery can't silently end with work unmerged or unarchived, right beside the other hook-enforced rules.
work_items.contract: README.md's 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph enumerates the plugin hook rules; add the delivery-end clause to that rule list — a session cannot silently end with built-but-unmerged items or a complete-but-unarchived binder; it is blocked once with the fix named, then the next identical stop passes. The clause stays plain-language and consistent with the doctrine wording used elsewhere. One consequential renumber: the existing '(a fifth hook lists your binders at session start)' parenthetical becomes '(a sixth hook lists your binders at session start)' — with the delivery-end rule added, the session-start injection is the sixth hook, and freezing the old count would leave the README lying about its own list. No other README change.

assertions: the 'Enforcement below the agent' paragraph's rule list gains a delivery-end clause: a session cannot silently end with built-but-unmerged items or a complete-but-unarchived binder — blocked once with the fix named, then the next identical stop passes
the session-start parenthetical is renumbered to 'a sixth hook', and no other section of README.md changes
